U
Oracle Events
Search QuickBooks for all client invoices (revenue side) using quickbooks_search_invoices, querying by customer for each core client. Find Canopy Health invoices: NMX-2026-0031 ($5,047 for Abdi relocation), INV-2026-0301 ($10,000 for 2 standard relocations + stipend), INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 for Q2 triple relocation with surcharges). Find Vectral Systems invoices: NMX-2026-0032 ($5,397 for Kevin Tran), INV-2026-0302 ($16,000 for 2 premium packages + stipend). Find GreenStack Energy invoices: NMX-2026-0033 ($7,289 for Priya Venkatesh including $650 electronics surcharge), INV-2026-0303 ($5,000 for 1 standard + stipend). Find PivotPoint Labs invoices: NMX-2026-0034 ($5,803 for Ines Kovac including walkup + electronics handling), INV-2026-0304 ($5,000 for 1 standard + stipend). Find BrightLoop Analytics: INV-2026-0305 ($10,000 for 2 standard relocations + stipend). Find Mosaic Robotics: INV-2026-0306 ($10,000 for 2 standard + stipend), INV-2026-0401 ($4,500 for Andre Washington).
Search QuickBooks for Heartland Movers vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills. Find bill_heartland_abdi ($2,150 for Abdi move, open — Balance equals TotalAmt indicating no payment applied), bill_heartland_q1_2026 ($12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each — the original bill), and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold ($12,800, same bill with DISPUTED status on 3 line items: Lily Marchetti $1,600 move cancelled Feb 10, Danielle Osei $1,600 move cancelled Feb 13, Priya Venkatesh $1,600 move handled by Swift not Heartland — valid amount is $8,000 for 5 moves, overbilling $4,800, payment on hold). Also find bill_heartland_q4_2025 ($6,400 for 4 Q4 moves, paid and clean) and bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 contingent liability accrual for Zara Okoye prototype damage, tagged DO NOT PAY, pending insurance resolution).
Search QuickBooks for Swift Relocations vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills. Find bill_swift_tran ($2,885 for Kevin Tran Portland to Denver including $85 bike handling), bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450 for Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle including $650 electronics/lab equipment surcharge), and bill_swift_kovac ($3,675 for Ines Kovac Brooklyn to Austin including $350 walkup + $125 electronics).
Search QuickBooks for other vendor bills and accruals using quickbooks_search_bills. Find bill_atlas_washington ($439.40 for Atlas Corporate Travel Andre Washington flight CMH to SEA), bill-aws-march-001 ($42,870 for AWS March 2026 — 3x spike from Terraform onboarding traffic), bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50,000 DOT/PHMSA contingent penalty accrual for hazmat compliance violation Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, one confirmed gap being the missing placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment), and BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 ($1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch on NorthWind account). Also find stipend bills on hold: STIP-2026-0094 ($89 Andre Washington duplicate receipt hash) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45 Jordan Ekwueme PDF template match), while Kevin Tran STIP-2026-0089 ($89) was already paid before the audit.
Search QuickBooks accounts for contingent liability balances using quickbooks_search_accounts. Find Contingent Liability - Damage Claims (acct_contingent_liability_claims) at $90,000 current balance for the Mosaic prototype damage, Regulatory Compliance — DOT Penalties (acct_dot_penalty_accrual) at $50,000 for the PHMSA compliance inquiry, Regulatory Compliance Expense (acct_regulatory_compliance_expense) at $50,000, and Claims & Remediation Expense (ACC-6185) at $1,200 for the KeyMove piano damage rider. The $90,000 Mosaic accrual has a critical detail: vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item under MoveOps policy, creating a $40,000 direct exposure gap if Heartland does not accept fault.
Search CRM for each core client company and their deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health", "Vectral Systems", "GreenStack Energy", "PivotPoint Labs", "BrightLoop Analytics", "Mosaic Robotics") and crm_search_deals to understand deal sizes and pipeline context. Find Canopy deal_canopy_q2 at $10,000 stage contractsent, Vectral deal_vectral_q2 at $11,000 stage contractsent with renewal context around Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount and counter at 10%, GreenStack deal_greenstack_q2 at $13,500 stage contractsent, PivotPoint deal_pivot_q2 at $5,803 stage closedwon, BrightLoop deal_brightloop_q2 at $7,000 stage qualifiedtobuy, Mosaic deal_mosaic_q2 at $4,500 stage closedwon, and Vantage Distributed (Terraform) at $1,850,000 stage closedwon.
Search Airtable for relocation records to cross-reference vendor assignments and move statuses using airtable_list_bases to find the base, airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table, then airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records. Key findings for Heartland/Swift reconciliation: rec_reloc_marchetti shows Lily Marchetti as Cancelled with vendor cost $0 (Heartland billed $1,600 — invalid), rec_reloc_osei shows Danielle Osei as Cancelled with vendor cost $0 (Heartland billed $1,600 — invalid), rec_reloc_venkatesh shows Priya Venkatesh as Completed by Swift with vendor cost $1,600 to Swift not Heartland (Heartland billed $1,600 — invalid, wrong vendor). Also find recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta showing Zara Okoye Completed with $1,200 crating surcharge, prototype damage, CM-2026-0415, $90K liability, $50K coverage cap, $40K gap, and recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle showing Keiko Tanaka In Progress with $800 hazmat surcharge, $450 storage, laser cutter Class 3B with DOT documentation gap.
Search emails for Heartland vendor dispute correspondence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or "vendor dispute" or "overbilling"). Find Jake Loomis's original Q1 invoice submission (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800), Chloe's internal review identifying $4,800 overbilling on 3 of 8 moves, Marcus's formal dispute email to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com with cancellation evidence, Jake Loomis's formal rate adjustment notice with 20% rate increase effective Q3 2026 tied to resolving the Q1 dispute, and Hana's expanded Heartland review finding additional discrepancies beyond the original 3 including Kevin Tran unauthorized $650 storage charge and Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch.
Search emails for Swift Relocations pricing changes using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "pricing" or "DOT surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's pricing update with 15% base rate increase on new bookings effective immediately plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, which directly impacts future margins.
Search emails for Mosaic damage claim context using search_emails (query: "prototype" or "damage" or "robotic arm" or "Mosaic"). Find Tamara Byrd's urgent email about damaged Unit #1 valued at $90,000, Emeka's incident report (Ref: HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE) detailing root cause and the $50K insurance cap vs $90K value creating a $40K gap along with credit memo CM-2026-0415 and $1,200 crating hold recommendation, and Hana's accounting entries email covering credit memo CM-2026-0415 and contingent liability accrual ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC at $90,000.
Search emails for DOT compliance exposure using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding"). Find Linda Castellano's formal compliance inquiry (PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting documentation for all 2026 hazmat shipments, Chloe's hazmat inventory identifying 4 shipments — Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, Keiko Tanaka laser cutter with missing signed placarding certificate, Raj Patel UPS batteries, Joaquín Reyes solar rig/oversize — Marcus's extension request and DOT granting extension to June 5, 2026, and Greg Pallone confirming the Tanaka placarding certificate (SR-2026-04-1538) will be provided.
Search emails for stipend fraud issues Dmitri flagged using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "duplicate" or "TechComfort" or "receipt" or "fraud"). Find Dmitri's audit email identifying 3 suspicious transactions: Kevin Tran/Vectral $89 ergonomic keyboard with identical SHA-256 hash matching Andre Washington already paid, Andre Washington/Mosaic $89 same keyboard same hash on hold, Jordan Ekwueme/BrightLoop $45 monitor stand with same PDF template metadata for total exposure of $223. Find Marcus's hold actions placing STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 on admin hold while Kevin Tran's $89 was already disbursed. Find verification requests sent to client HR contacts Rachel Whitfield for Vectral, Tamara Byrd for Mosaic, and Tessa Moreno for BrightLoop.
Search Slack for financial discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "margin" or "profitability" or "vendor cost" or "Heartland" filtering to #finance channel). Find Marcus's vendor payment hold post in #finance with full $12,800 hold and $4,800 overbilling breakdown across 3 disputed moves with corrected invoice requested for $8,000. Find Hana's reconciliation tracker update (VRECON-2026-Q1-HM) showing Billed $12,800, Verified $8,000, Discrepancy $4,800. Find Alejandro's own Q1 margin analysis post: Mina 38% average margin at $40,200 revenue, Catalina 32% at $64,800, Emeka 29% at $46,700, for total Q1 revenue of $151,700. Find Alejandro's Q3 vendor-cost exposure model noting data reconciliation problems across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM. Find Hana's Mosaic damage accrual entries and DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000).
Search Slack for stipend and DOT compliance discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "stipend" or "duplicate receipt" or "DOT" or "PHMSA"). Find Marcus's admin hold announcement covering STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme $45) on hold with Kevin Tran $89 already paid and total potential loss if all three fraudulent at $223. Find Hana's reconciliation tracker notes on STIP-2026-0089, 0094, and 0097 under investigation. Find Marcus's DOT exposure post citing $50K+ to $200K potential penalty range and Hana's DOT accrual entry ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 at $50K in the Regulatory Compliance account.
Search Linear for existing tracking issues related to Heartland dispute, DOT compliance, damage claim, and stipend fraud using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "vendor dispute" or "DOT" or "stipend" or "damage" or "margin"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342) "Vendor invoice reconciliation — Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)" In Progress assigned to Chloe Vance with labels vendor-dispute and heartland-movers, linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118) "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response" assigned to Marcus Thorne, issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (OPS #2) "DOT Docs — Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser" In Progress assigned to Chloe with missing signed placarding certificate, issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (OPS #5) "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations — URGENT" Todo assigned to Chloe, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (#220) "Stipend duplicate detection — receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring" Todo assigned to Dmitri Volkov with labels receipt-fraud, and linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (#214 Ops) "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM" assigned to Alejandro Fuentes.
Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com.
Look up the #finance Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Find #finance channel_id is C005.
Post the Q1 client profitability breakdown in #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005) with content covering per-client margin breakdown showing revenue invoiced vs vendor costs for each of the 6 core clients, Heartland dispute status with $4,800 overbilling on hold and corrected amount $8,000 of $12,800, Mosaic damage claim at $90,000 contingent liability with $50,000 insurance cap and $40,000 MoveOps exposure gap, DOT compliance exposure at $50,000 accrual for PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 with extension to June 5 2026, stipend fraud exposure at $223 total across 3 suspicious transactions, and flags for accounts where margins diverge from Alejandro's pricing model.
Send email to Marcus Thorne CC Elena Rostova with the Q1 profitability numbers using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: elena.rostova@moveops.com) with subject referencing Q1 client profitability report. Content should include per-client actual margins showing invoiced revenue minus vendor costs, summary of disputed and on-hold items including Heartland $4,800 overbilling and Mosaic $90K damage accrual with $40K uncovered and DOT $50K penalty exposure and stipend fraud $223, flags for any account where actual margins deviate from the pricing model, and total contingent liability exposure across all open items.
Create a Linear issue for unresolved items needing follow-up using linear_create_issue (team: Operations or Finance-related team) with title referencing Q1 profitability open items or unresolved financial exposure. Description should cover Heartland consolidated dispute still pending with corrected invoice not yet received and rate increase threat for Q3, Mosaic damage claim pending insurance adjudication with $40K gap unresolved, DOT compliance documentation due June 5 with Tanaka placarding certificate still being obtained, stipend verification responses pending from client HR contacts, and Hana's expanded Heartland review finding additional discrepancies including Kevin Tran storage $650 and Priya Nair shuttle fee beyond the original 3 disputed items.

Look up the #finance Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Find #finance channel_id is C005.
Post the Q1 client profitability breakdown in #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005) with content covering per-client margin breakdown showing revenue invoiced vs vendor costs for each of the 6 core clients, Heartland dispute status with $4,800 overbilling on hold and corrected amount $8,000 of $12,800, Mosaic damage claim at $90,000 contingent liability with $50,000 insurance cap and $40,000 MoveOps exposure gap, DOT compliance exposure at $50,000 accrual for PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 with extension to June 5 2026, stipend fraud exposure at $223 total across 3 suspicious transactions, and flags for accounts where margins diverge from Alejandro's pricing model.
Send email to Marcus Thorne CC Elena Rostova with the Q1 profitability numbers using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: elena.rostova@moveops.com) with subject referencing Q1 client profitability report. Content should include per-client actual margins showing invoiced revenue minus vendor costs, summary of disputed and on-hold items including Heartland $4,800 overbilling and Mosaic $90K damage accrual with $40K uncovered and DOT $50K penalty exposure and stipend fraud $223, flags for any account where actual margins deviate from the pricing model, and total contingent liability exposure across all open items.
Create a Linear issue for unresolved items needing follow-up using linear_create_issue (team: Operations or Finance-related team) with title referencing Q1 profitability open items or unresolved financial exposure. Description should cover Heartland consolidated dispute still pending with corrected invoice not yet received and rate increase threat for Q3, Mosaic damage claim pending insurance adjudication with $40K gap unresolved, DOT compliance documentation due June 5 with Tanaka placarding certificate still being obtained, stipend verification responses pending from client HR contacts, and Hana's expanded Heartland review finding additional discrepancies including Kevin Tran storage $650 and Priya Nair shuttle fee beyond the original 3 disputed items.
